demographics.occupation: office professional in an open-plan corporate workplace
demographics.living_situation: lives alone; works from home some days

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My head feels like it's in a vice, my jaw is locked, and the thought of going to work tomorrow makes me want to vomit.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The headache and jaw pain have been building for three days and are now so bad I can't concentrate or rest, and paracetamol and ibuprofen aren't touching it.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on gradually and got steadily worse over the past three days
symptom_narrative.character: a tight, band-like, squeezing pressure all around my head, like a vice; the jaw feels clenched and locked
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst headache I can remember having, and it just won't ease off
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all around my head, across the temples and the back, plus both sides of my jaw and my cheeks
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time, worse toward the end of the day and worst when I think about work; I've been grinding my teeth at night
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped — I've been taking paracetamol and ibuprofen for two days with no relief
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about going back to work, and clenching my jaw
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling sick to my stomach at the thought of work, can't focus, feel detached and on edge, exhausted
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get tension headaches sometimes but never this bad or this long, and never with the jaw locking up like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't concentrate on anything and I can't rest because of the pain; I've been taking sick days

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: occasional tension headaches in the past, usually settle with paracetamol

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: regular over-the-counter doses for the last 2 days
regular over-the-counter doses for the last 2 days

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Under a lot of stress at work in an open-plan office. Dreads the start of the work week, keeps replaying ambiguous interactions with colleagues, holds back how I really feel at work, feels isolated and self-doubting despite doing the job well. There is no easy way to leave the job right now.

objective_data_offered.value: on video she looks tense and uncomfortable, repeatedly rubbing her temples and the sides of her jaw, and becomes tearful
she says she can only open her mouth part-way because it feels locked, and it is sore over both jaw muscles
